one_liner: CELH up 5.8% in Wyckoff Accumulation A-B (2/5) with MACD bullish crossover — three analysts maintain Buy with targets averaging $50 (67% upside), DCF base case $45.79, trading at 8-year lows; the setup is forming, wait for BB upper breakout at $32.

price_action.intraday_story: CELH opened at $28.10, found support at $27.97 (just above the 90-day support at $27.74), then rallied steadily to close near the session high at $29.85. Average volume with a close at the upper end of the range — healthy accumulation-phase price action without exhaustion.

news_catalyst.headline: Roth, B of A, Morgan Stanley All Maintain Buy on Celsius — Targets $45-57 Despite Lowered Estimates
news_catalyst.summary: Three major institutional analysts simultaneously maintained Buy/Overweight ratings today while lowering price targets (Roth to $57, B of A to $45, Morgan Stanley to $48). All three lowered targets reflect near-term revenue estimate cuts, but none changed the fundamental bull thesis: CELH is a market share story in the energy drink category with PepsiCo distribution expanding globally. The fact that three separate houses maintained Buy at current prices (while the stock trades at 8-year lows per the media framing) is a strong institutional signal.

technical.summary: RSI at 50 is neutral with significant upside room. MACD in fresh bullish crossover. Wyckoff Phase A-B with 2/5 signals — earlier than LULU's Phase B. The $27.74 support held today and the close at $29.85 is above the BB middle. Breakout target: $32 (BB upper) then $36.24 (90-day resistance).

wyckoff_narrative: CELH is in the earlier stages of Wyckoff accumulation (Phase A-B vs LULU's Phase B). 2/5 signals detected means the SC and AR have likely been tagged. The stock needs more base-building before Phase C and the Spring/Shakeout that precedes markup. Today's bounce on institutional analyst backing is constructive but not yet a structural signal.

valuation.summary: 53% DCF upside to base case of $45.79 — among the strongest risk/reward in the watchlist. Bear case at $22.95 is 23% downside. Risk/reward of roughly 2.3:1 favors longs. Three institutional analysts with targets averaging $50 are aligned with the DCF base case.

key_strengths: 23% growth rate with PepsiCo distribution moat
Grade A — top-tier for consumer products
key_concerns: Consumer discretionary headwinds — energy drinks are semi-discretionary
Multiple analysts lowering targets signals near-term revenue disappointments ahead
fundamentals.summary: Grade A fundamentals with a 23% growth rate that institutional analysts believe is sustainable through the PepsiCo distribution expansion. The 8-year low price creates an attractive entry for the fundamental case.

risks: Near-term revenue disappointments expected — all three analysts lowered targets while maintaining Buy
Consumer spending compression from tariff/inflation headwinds disproportionately hits discretionary drink categories
Energy drink competition intensifying (Monster, Prime, Alani Nu)
